description:
You are a Linux systems engineer who has just received an INI-style configuration file that needs to be reviewed and hardened.

STARTING POINT
──────────────
1. The file to work on is:
   /home/user/appd/config.ini

2. The file already exists and contains several sections such as
   [core], [analytics], [network], [logging] and [beta].  
   • Inside every section there is at least the key/value pair  
     enabled=<true|false>  
     secure=<true|false>

REQUIREMENTS
────────────
Perform the following actions from the Linux shell.  
The entire job can be done with 5–10 reasonable shell commands and any text-processing tool you like (sed, awk, grep, python, etc.):

1. Back up the original file to:
   /home/user/backup/config.ini.bak  
   • The backup must be an *exact* byte-for-byte copy of the original before you change anything.

2. Add a single comment line that reads exactly
   # Hardened by SecOps  
   as the VERY FIRST line of /home/user/appd/config.ini  
   (It must appear only once and must stay at the top of the file).

3. Harden insecure sections:
   • If a section has the key secure=false (case-sensitive comparison), set the key enabled to false in that same section, regardless of its previous value.  
   • Leave every other key/value untouched.

4. Append a NEW section to the bottom of the file that looks exactly like this (including quotation marks and spacing):
   [compliance]
   enabled=true
   secure=true
   auditor="sysop"

5. Generate a human-readable report file at:
   /home/user/output/config_report.log

   The report must:
   • Contain ONE line per section that existed **before** you added [compliance].  
   • Lines must be sorted alphabetically by section name.  
   • Each line must follow this strict format (no spaces except the single ones shown here):
     Section=<SECTION_NAME>; Enabled=<VALUE>; Secure=<VALUE>
     Example: Section=core; Enabled=true; Secure=true

   The automated checker will read the file and must match the format *exactly*, including character case.

6. Do not remove or rename any other files or directories.

7. When you believe you are done, the following files must exist and be world-readable (mode 0644 is fine):
   • /home/user/backup/config.ini.bak  
   • /home/user/appd/config.ini (modified)  
   • /home/user/output/config_report.log

You may print any console output you find helpful while working, but make sure the final state of the filesystem satisfies everything above. The automated tests will inspect file paths, permissions, and contents verbatim.
truth:
Initial contents of /home/user/appd/config.ini BEFORE the agent touches it:

[core]
enabled=true
secure=true
timeout=30

[analytics]
enabled=true
secure=false
sample_rate=10

[network]
enabled=true
secure=true
port=8080

[logging]
enabled=true
secure=false
level=debug

[beta]
enabled=true
secure=false
experimental=true


Expected FINAL contents of /home/user/appd/config.ini AFTER all steps:

# Hardened by SecOps
[core]
enabled=true
secure=true
timeout=30

[analytics]
enabled=false
secure=false
sample_rate=10

[network]
enabled=true
secure=true
port=8080

[logging]
enabled=false
secure=false
level=debug

[beta]
enabled=false
secure=false
experimental=true

[compliance]
enabled=true
secure=true
auditor="sysop"

— Note: only the 'enabled=' lines inside [analytics], [logging], and [beta] changed from true → false.

Contents expected in /home/user/output/config_report.log (each line terminated with a single \n):

Section=analytics; Enabled=false; Secure=false
Section=beta; Enabled=false; Secure=false
Section=core; Enabled=true; Secure=true
Section=logging; Enabled=false; Secure=false
Section=network; Enabled=true; Secure=true

There are exactly 5 lines, sorted alphabetically by section name.

Verification points:
• /home/user/backup/config.ini.bak must be byte-identical to the pre-modification file shown above.
• First line of the working config must be literally "# Hardened by SecOps".
• The [compliance] section must appear at the bottom with the exact three key/value pairs as shown.
• All other key/value pairs remain unchanged except the three enabled= edits.
• The report file must match the six conditions in the task description, especially format and sort order.
name: task_000000_20795c54
